risks.json.title: Data Loss through Ransomware
risks.json.description: Encryption of business-critical data through ransomware attack. Affects production systems, ERP and backup infrastructure.
risks.json.vulnerability: Lack of network segmentation, insufficient offline backups

risks.json.title: Unauthorised Use of Workstation
risks.json.description: When leaving the workstation, the computer is not locked with a password, allowing unauthorised persons to gain access.
risks.json.vulnerability: Lack of clean desk policy, insufficient awareness

risks.json.title: Phishing / CEO Fraud
risks.json.description: Targeted spear-phishing attacks on executives and finance department to initiate fraudulent transfers or credential theft.
risks.json.vulnerability: Lack of multi-factor authentication, insufficient training

risks.json.title: Failure of Critical Cloud Infrastructure (AWS)
risks.json.description: Total failure of the AWS region eu-central-1 leads to complete outage of all production infrastructure. No sufficient multi-region redundancy in place.
risks.json.vulnerability: Single-region deployment, lack of failover automation

risks.json.title: Insider Threat from Privileged Users
risks.json.description: Administrators or developers with excessive access rights could intentionally or negligently exfiltrate data or manipulate systems.
risks.json.vulnerability: Lack of least-privilege enforcement, insufficient Privileged Access Management (PAM)

risks.json.title: Vulnerabilities in Deployed Open-Source Software
risks.json.description: Critical CVEs in open-source libraries (Log4j scenario) can remain undetected in production systems and be exploited for remote code execution.
risks.json.vulnerability: No Software Composition Analysis (SCA) process, missing SBOM

risks.json.title: AI-Assisted System Makes Discriminatory Decisions
risks.json.description: An AI system used in-house for pre-screening job applications may make discriminatory decisions and potentially violates EU AI Act Art. 5.
risks.json.vulnerability: Lack of AI risk classification, no AI Act compliance process

risks.json.title: Data Breach through Misconfigured Cloud Storage
risks.json.description: Publicly accessible S3 buckets or Azure blob containers can inadvertently expose personal data (GDPR violation, reporting obligation).
risks.json.threat: Data breach, GDPR violation, reputational loss
risks.json.vulnerability: Lack of Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) tools, no automated config scanning

risks.json.title: Core Processor Failure due to Natural Disaster
risks.json.description: Flooding or fire at the main data centre leads to complete failure of the data centre without sufficient fallback capability.
risks.json.vulnerability: No geo-redundant data centre, outdated BCM plan

risks.json.title: Supplier Compromise (Supply Chain Attack)
risks.json.description: A critical software supplier (e.g. SAP partner, network equipment vendor) is compromised and distributes malware via legitimate update channels (SolarWinds scenario).
risks.json.vulnerability: Lack of supplier security assessment, no integrity check of updates

risks.json.title: Breach of NIS2 Reporting Obligation
risks.json.description: A security incident is not reported within the required 24-hour early warning to the competent national authority (BSI), leading to significant fines.
risks.json.threat: Regulatory sanction, fines up to EUR 10 million or 2% of annual turnover
risks.json.vulnerability: No documented NIS2 reporting process, unclear responsibilities

risks.json.title: Data Loss due to Missing Backup Validation
risks.json.description: Backups are created but not regularly checked for recoverability. In an emergency, backups may be corrupt or incomplete.
risks.json.vulnerability: No backup recovery test process, missing monitoring alerting

goals.json.title: Increase Patch Rate to 95 %
goals.json.description: Ensure at least 95 % of all systems have critical patches applied within 30 days of release.

goals.json.title: Deploy MFA for All Admin Accounts
goals.json.description: Enable multi-factor authentication for all privileged user accounts across all systems and cloud services.

goals.json.title: Achieve ISO 27001 Certification
goals.json.description: Successfully pass ISO 27001:2022 Stage 1 and Stage 2 audits and obtain certification by end of 2026.

goals.json.title: Reduce Phishing Click Rate Below 5 %
goals.json.description: Through regular phishing simulations and awareness training, reduce employee phishing click rate to under 5 %.

training.json.title: Security Awareness Fundamentals
training.json.description: Annual mandatory training for all employees.

training.json.title: GDPR Training for New Employees
training.json.description: Introduction to data protection obligations pursuant to Art. 39 GDPR.
training.json.evidence: Participant list and certificate stored in SharePoint.

training.json.title: ISO 27001 Lead Auditor Certification
training.json.description: External certification training for ISMS managers.

bia.title: ERP System SAP S/4HANA
bia.notes: Core process: order management, financial accounting, human resources. Outage leads directly to production standstill.

bia.title: Production Line Plant A
bia.notes: Highest criticality — immediate revenue loss on outage. RTO=2h, RPO=0 (no data loss acceptable).

bia.title: Email & Communications (M365)
bia.notes: Outage affects all departments. Fallback: mobile phones, emergency contact list.

bia.title: Personnel Master Data & Payroll
bia.notes: Critical for payroll runs (monthly). Legal deadline: salary payment by month end.

bia.title: Accounting & Financial Reporting
bia.notes: Critical for monthly/annual close, liquidity management and statutory reporting obligations.

bia.title: Building Management System (BMS)
bia.notes: Outage affects server room air conditioning (→ overheating risk), fire protection and access. Highest priority.

bia.title: Customer Service CRM
bia.notes: Impacts customer support, but no immediate production outage. BIA assessment not yet fully completed.

bia.title: Supply Chain Portal
bia.notes: Outage delays ordering processes and just-in-time deliveries. Manual fallback processes documented.

plans.title: IT Recovery Plan (ITP) SAP
plans.scope: SAP S/4HANA, SAP PI/PO, SAP BW – all production clients
plans.procedures:
1. Activate IT emergency team
2. Failover to DR data centre (DC2)
3. SAP restart according to priority list
4. Functional test of core processes
5. Sign-off by CIO
6. Communication to business departments

plans.title: Business Continuity Plan Production
plans.scope: Production line Plant A and B – all shifts
plans.procedures:
1. Alert plant manager and emergency team
2. Assess damage extent
3. Activate alternate production (Plant B)
4. Coordination with logistics / customers
5. Damage remediation and restart

plans.title: Cyber Attack / Ransomware Emergency Plan
plans.scope: Entire IT infrastructure including OT network
plans.procedures:
1. Isolate affected systems
2. Activate incident response team
3. Notify BSI / authorities (if reportable)
4. Forensics and damage assessment
5. Restore from offline backups
6. Post-incident review

plans.title: Crisis Communication Plan
plans.scope: Internal and external corporate communications during crisis events
plans.procedures:
1. Activate crisis committee
2. Initial internal notification (email + MS Teams)
3. External communication (press release, social media hold)
4. Customer communication (key account managers)
5. Authority communication (if required)

plans.title: Building Evacuation & Emergency BMS
plans.scope: All buildings and sites including server rooms
plans.procedures:
1. Trigger fire alarm / manual alert
2. Evacuation following escape route plan
3. Assembly points and headcount
4. Emergency cooling for server room
5. Clearance by fire brigade

plans.title: Cloud Outage (Azure) DRP
plans.procedures: Draft in progress. Planned measures: on-premise fallback for critical services, offline mail access, alternative DNS resolution.

plans.title: HR Failure Emergency Handbook
plans.procedures:
1. Manual time recording via Excel template
2. Payroll via external service provider (DATEV direct)
3. Paper-based personnel records as fallback
4. Escalation to CFO if outage > 24h

exercises.title: Tabletop Exercise Ransomware Scenario
exercises.findings: Communication chain worked well. BSI reporting process was correctly run through. Improvement needed for offline backup access.
exercises.actions: Update offline backup procedure (by 2025-11-30). Supplement BSI contact list.

exercises.title: Full Drill Production Outage Plant A
exercises.findings: Alternate production switchover to Plant B took 45 min longer than planned (RTO target 2h not met). Cause: tool machine approval process unclear. Documentation outdated.
exercises.actions: Update BCP Production – document Plant B approval process (by 2026-01-15). Plan additional training for shift supervisors.

exercises.title: Walkthrough IT Recovery SAP
exercises.findings: All steps of the ITP correctly documented. Failover to DC2 in 3h 10min (within RTO 4h). Role assignments clear.
exercises.actions: No critical actions. Contact list for DC2 operator updated (done).

exercises.title: Tabletop Exercise Power Outage Data Centre
exercises.findings: UPS switchover and diesel generator startup correctly exercised. Communication to executives works.
exercises.actions: No open actions. Plan next exercise with real diesel generator test.

exercises.title: Planned Crisis Committee Simulation
exercises.actions: Complete scenario preparation by 2026-05-15. Commission external facilitation.

exercises.title: Planned Full Drill BCP Production
exercises.actions: Finalise exercise plan by 2026-07-01. Simulate alternate production Plant B including time measurement.

assets.json.name: ERP System (SAP S/4HANA)
assets.json.description: Central ERP system for financial accounting, controlling, procurement and production. Operated as SaaS via SAP Business Technology Platform.
assets.json.notes: Critical system – SLA 99.9% agreed. Emergency plan in place.

assets.json.name: Production Server Plant 1
assets.json.description: Physical rack server for production control and MES system at Plant Dortmund. Redundant configuration (cluster with backup NAS).
assets.json.notes: Maintenance contract until 2026. EoL evaluation Q4/2025 planned.

assets.json.name: Active Directory / LDAP
assets.json.description: Microsoft Active Directory Domain Services and LDAP directory for the entire group structure. Central identity and access management (IAM).
assets.json.notes: Tier-0 asset. Privileged access exclusively via PAM system. Quarterly review.

assets.json.name: Customer Database CRM
assets.json.description: PostgreSQL database with customer master data, sales history and contact data. Approx. 850,000 records, of which 240,000 with personal data (GDPR-relevant).
assets.json.notes: RoPA entry in place (Art. 30 GDPR). Encryption at-rest and in-transit enabled. Daily backup.

assets.json.name: Firewall Cluster (Perimeter)
assets.json.description: Fortinet FortiGate HA cluster as central perimeter firewall of the group. Separates internet, DMZ and internal network. NGFW with IPS/IDS and SSL inspection.
assets.json.location: Data Centre Frankfurt, Zone A, Rack A-01/A-02
assets.json.notes: HA cluster active/passive. Failover time < 10 s. Annual penetration test.

assets.json.name: Microsoft 365 (E3 Licensing)
assets.json.description: Microsoft 365 E3 for all group employees: Exchange Online, Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive, Office apps. Data residency EU (Netherlands). DLP policies configured.
assets.json.notes: Tenant ID documented. Conditional Access enabled. MFA mandatory for all users. DPA with Microsoft in place (GDPR).

assets.json.name: Data Centre Frankfurt (Primary DC)
assets.json.description: Primary data centre in Frankfurt am Main. Tier 3 certified, redundant power supply (UPS + emergency generator), N+1 cooling, biometric + badge access.
assets.json.notes: ISO 27001 certification in place. Annual inspection + audit. Emergency supply secured for 72 h.

assets.json.name: Managed SOC (External)
assets.json.description: External Security Operations Centre by Telekom Security GmbH. 24/7 SIEM monitoring, incident detection & response. SLA: P1 incidents < 15 min response time.
assets.json.notes: DPA (GDPR Art. 28) in place. Annual service review. Log data not stored outside the EU.

suppliers.json.name: Microsoft Deutschland GmbH
suppliers.json.description: Microsoft services for cloud, collaboration and productivity. Core supplier for email, video conferencing, office suite and Azure cloud infrastructure. Certified to ISO 27001, SOC 2 and BSI C5.
suppliers.json.products: Microsoft 365 (Exchange, Teams, SharePoint), Azure Cloud Services, Microsoft Defender
suppliers.json.notes: Critical cloud provider. DPA concluded via Microsoft Data Protection Addendum (DPA). Standard contractual clauses (SCC) agreed for third-country transfers. DPIA conducted and documented. Annual review of compliance reports (Audit Report M365).

suppliers.json.name: DATEV eG
suppliers.json.description: Tax advisory and accounting software for SMEs. Processes highly sensitive financial and payroll data. Cooperative with its own ISO 27001 certification and regular IDW PS 951 audits.
suppliers.json.products: DATEV Unternehmen online, Payroll & Salary, DATEV DMS
suppliers.json.notes: Processes sensitive payroll and financial data. DPA concluded pursuant to GDPR Art. 28. Annual review of IDW PS 951 report. DATEV is itself ISO 27001 certified (certificate verified 2025-09-15).

suppliers.json.name: Deutsche Telekom AG
suppliers.json.description: Telecommunications service provider for internet, SD-WAN and telephony. Core infrastructure for network connectivity. NIS2-regulated operator of critical infrastructure.
suppliers.json.products: Business Internet (1 Gbit/s), SD-WAN, IP Telephony, M2M Services
suppliers.json.notes: No direct access to business data. SLA monitoring via Telekom Business customer portal. Fault notifications and escalation paths contractually agreed. First supplier audit planned for Q2 2026.

suppliers.json.name: SAP SE
suppliers.json.description: ERP system manufacturer. SAP S/4HANA is the central core business system for finance, procurement, production and HR. Business-critical – an outage would completely halt business operations.
suppliers.json.products: SAP S/4HANA On-Premise, SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform), SAP SuccessFactors
suppliers.json.notes: Highest criticality – business-critical for all operations. Annual security review conducted with SAP audit team. Emergency plan (BCP) for SAP failure documented in BCM module. RTO: 4h, RPO: 1h.

suppliers.json.name: Hetzner Online GmbH
suppliers.json.description: German hosting and cloud provider. Operates dedicated servers and cloud VMs for test and development environments. Data centres exclusively in Germany and Finland (EU).
suppliers.json.products: Dedicated Root Server, Hetzner Cloud (CX series), Hetzner Object Storage
suppliers.json.notes: Data centres in Falkenstein/Nuremberg (DE) and Helsinki (FI) – fully EU-based. DPA concluded via Hetzner data processing agreement. BSI C5 attestation applied for, result expected Q2 2026.

suppliers.json.name: Securelink GmbH
suppliers.json.description: IT security service provider for penetration tests and ISMS consulting. Conducts annual penetration tests of the entire IT infrastructure and web applications. Supports the ISO 27001 certification.
suppliers.json.products: Penetration tests (infrastructure, web, social engineering), ISMS consulting, ISO 27001 preparation
suppliers.json.notes: NDA concluded (nda_1772961679125_rhz7). Penetration test result 2025: 3 critical, 7 high vulnerabilities found and remediated. Next test: December 2026. Consultant background checks conducted annually.

suppliers.json.name: Cisco Systems GmbH
suppliers.json.description: Network infrastructure manufacturer. Supplies switches, firewalls (Cisco ASA/Firepower) and WLAN infrastructure. Supply chain integrity of hardware is ensured via Cisco Trust Anchor technology.
suppliers.json.products: Catalyst Switches (9000 series), Firepower NGFW, Meraki WLAN, Cisco ISE
suppliers.json.notes: No direct data access. Hardware integrity verified via Cisco Trust Anchor Module (TAm). PSIRT advisories monitored monthly and patches applied promptly. Maintenance contract with 4h NBD support.

suppliers.json.name: AWS EMEA SARL
suppliers.json.description: Hyperscaler cloud platform for production infrastructure. Operates critical production workloads (EC2, RDS, S3). Status 'under_review': GDPR third-country review pursuant to ECJ case law in progress.
suppliers.json.products: Amazon EC2, S3, RDS (PostgreSQL), CloudFront, AWS WAF, Lambda
suppliers.json.notes: Main infrastructure in Frankfurt (eu-central-1) – fully EU-based. AWS DPA and SCC concluded. Transfer Impact Assessment (TIA) conducted for US data transfers. Status 'under_review': Legal review of GDPR compliance pursuant to current ECJ case law in progress (since 2025-11). Result expected Q1 2026.

suppliers.json.name: TÜV Rheinland AG
suppliers.json.description: Accredited certification body for ISO 27001. Conducts external ISMS audits (Stage 1 + Stage 2) and issues the ISO 27001 certificate. Next recertification: September 2026.
suppliers.json.products: ISO 27001 Certification (Stage 1 + Stage 2 Audit), Surveillance Audit, Recertification Audit
securityRequirements: NDA for audit information and test results
suppliers.json.notes: External auditor for ISO 27001 recertification 2026 (3-year cycle). Last certificate issued: 2023-09-15, valid until 2026-09-14. NDA for audit documentation concluded. Surveillance audits 2024 and 2025 passed successfully.

suppliers.json.name: Lexware GmbH & Co. KG
suppliers.json.description: Accounting and payroll software (Haufe Group). No longer actively used – migration to DATEV completed. Licence expires 2026-12-31, then contract ends.
suppliers.json.products: Lexware financial office premium, Lexware Payroll
suppliers.json.notes: Replaced by DATEV (migration completed Oct. 2025). Licence runs until 2026-12-31 – no further audit planned. Data migration complete, remaining data will be archived until contract end, then deleted (Art. 17 GDPR). DPA termination notice submitted.

reviews.title: Management Review 2024 – Annual Review
reviews.participants: Claudia Meier (CISO), Petra Vogel (DPO), Thomas Becker (CTO), Sandra Hoffmann (CFO), Markus Schneider (IT Manager)
reviews.inputAuditResults: Internal ISO 27001 audit Q3/2024: 3 minor nonconformities identified (access rights review, patch management documentation, supplier security clauses). All findings were recorded as action items. External certification audit planned for Q4/2025.
reviews.inputStakeholderFeedback: Customer survey 2024: 94% satisfaction with data protection practices. 2 customers cited ISO 27001 certificate as a selection criterion. No major complaints. Supervisory authority BayLDA: No ongoing proceedings.
reviews.inputPerformance: Security objectives 2024: 7 of 9 objectives achieved. Awareness training rate: 87% (target: 90%). Patch compliance: 94% (target: 95%). Average incident response time: 2.3h (target: 4h – exceeded). No critical data breaches in the reporting period.
reviews.inputNonconformities: 3 minor nonconformities from internal audit (see above). 1 data breach (internal report accidentally sent to wrong recipient) – reported and documented pursuant to Art. 33 GDPR, not a notifiable event. Remediation measures completed.
reviews.inputPreviousActions: Action plan from Review 2023: 12 of 14 actions completed (86%). Open: ICS network segmentation (in progress, 65%), Business Continuity Plan update (deferred to 2025).
reviews.inputRisksOpportunities: Risk assessment 2024: 2 new critical risks identified (supply chain attacks, AI-assisted phishing attacks). Overall risk profile: 3 critical, 8 high, 15 medium. Opportunity: Cloud migration enables centralised logging and improved monitoring.
reviews.inputExternalChanges: EU AI Act adopted – implications for internal AI applications under review. NIS2 Implementation Act in force – scope analysis completed (company is affected as an essential entity). GDPR fine practice: increased risk due to stronger supervisory activity.
reviews.decisions: 1. ISMS scope remains unchanged for 2025. 2. Budget for recertification audit 2025 approved (€45,000). 3. ICS security programme to be equipped with additional resources. 4. Awareness training made mandatory for all employees (including executives). 5. External penetration test engagement for Q1/2025 agreed.
reviews.improvements: Introduction of an automated patch management tool (Q1/2025). Revision of supplier security clauses in all new contracts. Establishment of a SIEM system for improved threat detection. Implementation of MFA for all privileged accounts.
reviews.resourceNeeds: Additional 0.5 FTE for ISMS coordination approved. Budget for SIEM solution: €120,000 p.a. Penetration test budget: €30,000. Training budget increased by 20% to €48,000.
reviews.notes: Next management review planned for 14 Nov 2025. Interim review Q1/2025 focusing on NIS2 compliance to be conducted by CISO.

reviews.title: Interim Review Q1/2025 – NIS2 & Recertification Preparation
reviews.participants: Markus Schneider (IT Manager), Petra Vogel (DPO), Julia Brandt (HR), Thomas Becker (CTO)
reviews.inputAuditResults: External penetration test Q1/2025: 2 high findings (insufficient MFA enforcement on 3 systems, outdated TLS configuration on web servers), 5 medium findings. Action plan created.
reviews.inputStakeholderFeedback: NIS2 authority contact: Registration obligation confirmed by 30 Sep 2025. No negative customer feedback. Internal employee data protection survey: improvement needed in awareness.
reviews.inputPerformance: Current awareness training rate: 42% (Q2 target: 75%). Penetration test finding remediation: 60%. ISO 27001 recertification audit preparation: 40% complete.
reviews.inputNonconformities: 2 high findings from penetration test classified as temporary nonconformities. Remediation deadline: 30 Apr 2025.
reviews.inputPreviousActions: Of 5 priority actions from annual review: 2 completed, 3 in progress. SIEM implementation delayed (supplier issue), new deadline: Q3/2025.
reviews.inputRisksOpportunities: New risk: AI-assisted phishing campaigns (assessment: critical). Supply chain risk substantiated by 2 known incidents in the industry.
reviews.inputExternalChanges: BSI IT-Grundschutz Compendium Edition 2025 published – gap analysis initiated. Data protection supervisory authority increasing audit activities in the healthcare sector.
reviews.decisions: 1. MFA enforcement for all privileged accounts by 30 Apr 2025. 2. Awareness training to start immediately (Julia Brandt responsible). 3. SIEM supplier change to be reviewed. 4. Ensure NIS2 registration by 30 Sep 2025.
reviews.improvements: Update TLS configuration on all web servers. Roll out MFA for privileged accounts.
reviews.resourceNeeds: No additional resources required. Prioritisation of existing tasks.
reviews.notes: Next regular management review scheduled as planned on 07 Nov 2025.

reviews.title: Management Review 2025 – Annual Review
reviews.notes: Date reserved. Preparation begins October 2025. Agenda to be prepared by CISO by 01 Oct 2025.

actions.title: Production Network Penetration Test
actions.description: Commissioning and execution of an external penetration test for the production network including web applications and API endpoints. Scope: all production-critical systems.
actions.notes: Service provider selected (SRC Security). Kick-off meeting on 15 Apr 2025. Scoping completed.

actions.title: Awareness Training All Employees (Mandatory 2025)
actions.description: Execution of the annual security awareness training for all employees including executives. Format: e-learning + in-person sessions for high-risk groups. Completion quiz with minimum 80% score.
actions.notes: E-learning platform still needs to be configured. Content is ready.

actions.title: Complete ICS Network Segmentation
actions.description: Full separation of the OT/ICS network from the IT network via firewall rules and DMZ. Implementation according to IEC 62443 zone model. All legacy connections must be documented and terminated if necessary.
actions.notes: OVERDUE. Original deadline exceeded. Technical obstacles with legacy PLC connections. New deadline to be agreed with management.

actions.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment CRM System
actions.description: Conduct a DPIA pursuant to Art. 35 GDPR for the new CRM system (Salesforce). Assessment of risks to data subjects, action plan for risk reduction.
actions.notes: Awaiting completion of data mapping documentation by IT.

actions.title: Update Business Continuity Plan
actions.description: Revision and update of the BCP/DRP in line with the changed IT landscape (cloud migration). Including tabletop exercise with all responsible parties.
actions.notes: Resources not available until Q2/2025 (after cloud migration completion).

actions.title: Introduce Supplier Security Audits
actions.description: Establish a structured supplier audit programme: questionnaire, risk classification, annual planning. Audit at least top 10 suppliers in the first year.
actions.notes: Questionnaire created, 11 suppliers assessed. Result: 2 suppliers with elevated risk identified, improvement measures agreed.

actions.title: Revise SIEM Alerting Rules
actions.description: Revision and optimisation of SIEM correlation rules following security incident in Feb 2025. Reduction of false positives, improvement of detection rate for known attack patterns (MITRE ATT&CK).
actions.notes: 50 of 67 rules revised. Testing phase underway. Sign-off by CISO pending.

actions.title: Prepare ISO 27001 Recertification Audit
actions.description: Full preparation for the ISO 27001 recertification audit (Stage 1 + Stage 2). Internal gap analysis, document review, employee training, mock audit by external experts.
actions.notes: Project plan created. Kick-off meeting on 01 Apr 2025. Certification body TÜV Rheinland engaged.

meetings.title: ISMS Committee Q4/2024
meetings.participants: Dr. Klaus Müller (CEO), Petra Vogel (DPO), Markus Schneider (IT Manager), Thomas Becker (CTO), Julia Brandt (HR), Sandra Hoffmann (CFO)
meetings.agenda:
1. Approval of Q3/2024 minutes
2. Status of action plan Management Review 2024
3. Internal penetration test results
4. NIS2 scope analysis – presentation of results
5. ISMS budget 2025
6. Any other business
meetings.decisions:
1. Q3/2024 minutes approved.
2. Management Review 2024 action plan noted. ICS segmentation prioritisation confirmed.
3. Penetration test findings: budget for immediate remediation of 2 high findings approved (€15,000).
4. NIS2 scope confirmed. Registration obligation by 30 Sep 2025. Responsible: CISO.
5. ISMS budget 2025 approved: €280,000 (including SIEM, training, recertification).
meetings.notes: Next meeting: 11 Mar 2025 (Q1/2025). Minutes sent by email to all participants on 10 Dec 2024.

meetings.title: CISO Weekly CW10/2025
meetings.participants: Markus Schneider (IT Manager), Kevin Müller (SOC), Lisa Wagner (Security Engineering)
meetings.agenda:
1. SOC weekly report (incidents, alerts, anomalies)
2. Status of ongoing actions (SIEM rules, MFA rollout)
3. Penetration test preparation
4. Any other business
meetings.decisions:
1. SOC: 3 false-positive alerts from last week analysed, rules adjusted.
2. MFA rollout: 78% complete. Remaining accounts by 15 Mar 2025.
3. Pentest: finalise scoping document by 14 Mar 2025.

meetings.title: Risk Management Committee February 2025
meetings.participants: Sandra Hoffmann (CFO), Thomas Becker (CTO), Petra Vogel (DPO), Klaus Richter (OT), Markus Schneider (IT Manager)
meetings.agenda:
1. Risk register update Q1/2025
2. New risks: AI-assisted attacks, supply chain
3. ICS risk situation (presentation Klaus Richter)
4. Risk tolerance adjustment
5. Any other business
meetings.decisions:
1. 2 new critical risks (AI phishing, supply chain) added to risk register. Treatment plans by 31 Mar 2025.
2. ICS risk: escalated to CEO. Additional resources for ICS segmentation approved.
3. Risk tolerance unchanged (score >15 = critical).
4. Next risk assessment: May 2025.
meetings.notes: ICS risk escalation communicated separately to management. Minutes approved on 25 Feb 2025.

public-incidents.json.description: An employee clicked on a fake Microsoft login link and entered their credentials. The link was received via email that appeared to originate from the IT department.
public-incidents.json.measuresTaken: Password immediately reset, account temporarily locked, email headers analysed.
public-incidents.json.cisoNotes: Phishing campaign still active – additional recipients identified.

public-incidents.json.description: Three file servers at the logistics branch were encrypted by ransomware. Ransom note with Bitcoin demand of EUR 85,000 left behind. Affected systems: \FS-LOG-01, FS-LOG-02, FS-LOG-03.
public-incidents.json.measuresTaken: Systems disconnected from network, backup team notified, BSI notification prepared.
public-incidents.json.cisoNotes: Critical. Recovery from backup in progress. BSI notification to follow within 72h.

public-incidents.json.description: A former employee logged into the VPN using old credentials and accessed the production ERP system. According to logs, bill-of-materials and costing data were retrieved.
public-incidents.json.measuresTaken: Access blocked, logs secured, line manager notified.
public-incidents.json.cisoNotes: Criminal complaint filed by legal department. GDPR reporting obligation assessed – data subjects notified.

public-incidents.json.description: Personnel files of 3 employees including salary information, medical records and application documents were accidentally sent by email to a wrong recipient (external job applicant).
public-incidents.json.measuresTaken: Recipient contacted, file deletion confirmed, internal notification documented.
public-incidents.json.cisoNotes: Art. 33 GDPR notification filed with supervisory authority on 16 Feb 2026. No sanction expected.

public-incidents.json.description: CEO fraud attempt: assistant received an email apparently from the CEO requesting an immediate transfer of EUR 47,000 to a new supplier account. Transfer was fortunately not executed.
public-incidents.json.measuresTaken: Transaction stopped, CEO contacted directly by phone – fraud confirmed, police report filed.
public-incidents.json.cisoNotes: Awareness training scheduled for all assistants and finance department.

public-incidents.json.description: EDR system identified an infostealer (Redline Stealer) on 2 workstations in the development area. Browser passwords and session tokens may be compromised.
public-incidents.json.measuresTaken: Systems isolated, EDR quarantine active, passwords of all affected users reset.
public-incidents.json.cisoNotes: Forensic image created. Checking whether tokens for cloud services were misused.

public-incidents.json.description: An unknown person gained physical access to the server room at the Stuttgart branch. Access control log shows a deleted entry. USB device connected to backup server.
public-incidents.json.measuresTaken: Server room locked, USB port blocker activated, access card deactivated, police notified.
public-incidents.json.cisoNotes: Video footage under review. IT forensics investigating USB activity.

public-incidents.json.description: Mass phishing campaign targeting sales staff: 14 persons opened fake DocuSign links. 3 of them entered their credentials.
public-incidents.json.measuresTaken: Affected accounts locked, MFA enforced, phishing URL reported and blocked.
public-incidents.json.cisoNotes: No data loss confirmed. Training scheduled for all sales staff.

public-incidents.json.description: Anonymous report: an employee allegedly copies customer data to a private USB stick on a regular basis and processes it from home. No further details about the affected department.
A database dump of the CRM database (approx. 12,000 customer records including contact and contract data) was discovered on a public paste service. Origin unclear.
public-incidents.json.measuresTaken: Paste site reported and taken down, database access analysed over the past 3 months, external specialist engaged.
public-incidents.json.cisoNotes: GDPR Art. 33/34 – notification to supervisory authority and notification of affected customers being prepared.

guidance.json.title: Demo Mode – Overview & Transition to Production
guidance.json.content:
# Demo Mode – Overview & Transition to Production

> This article appears automatically while the system is running in demo mode.
> It explains the available demo data, credentials, and the path to production.

---

## Demo Credentials

| Username | Password    | Role          | Domain | Notes                               |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| admin    | adminpass   | Administrator | Global | Full access, CISO + DSO function    |
| alice    | alicepass   | Department Head | IT   | Access to Guidance & Risks          |
| bob      | bobpass     | Reader        | HR     | Read-only access                    |

> **Security notice:** These passwords are publicly known. All passwords must be changed before going into production.

---

## Available Demo Data

The system contains realistic sample data for the following modules:

| Module | Demo Content |
|---|---|
| **Policies (Templates)** | Information Security Policy, Password Policy, BYOD Policy, Backup Policy, Access Control Policy (each as Draft / Review / Approved) |
| **SoA** | 313 controls across 8 frameworks (ISO 27001, BSI, NIS2, EUCS, EUAI, ISO 9001, CRA) — all editable |
| **Risk Management** | 12 realistic risks with multi-framework links (Ransomware, Phishing, Insider Threat, Supply Chain Attack, etc.) |
| **GDPR & Privacy** | Record of Processing Activities (RoPA), Data Processing Agreements (DPA), TOMs, DPIA entries, 72h timer demo |
| **Assets** | 8 enterprise assets (servers, workstations, ERP, cloud services, network infrastructure) with classification |
| **Supply Chain** | 6 suppliers (Microsoft, DATEV, SAP, Cisco, AWS EMEA, Hetzner) incl. NIS2/EUCS links |
| **BCM / BCP** | 8 Business Impact Analyses, 7 Continuity Plans, 6 Exercises |
| **Governance** | 3 Management Reviews with action items and meeting minutes |
| **Training** | 3 training measures (ISO Awareness, GDPR, Phishing Simulation) |
| **Legal** | 3 contracts, 2 NDAs, 2 privacy policies |
| **Security Goals** | 4 KPI objectives with progress bars (Vulnerability Response, Patch Compliance, Phishing Rate, Awareness) |
| **Incidents (Inbox)** | 10 demo reports from the public incident reporting form |
| **Guidance** | System manual, roles documentation, policy processes, SoA audit guide |

---

## Transitioning to Production

### Step by Step

1. **Open Admin Console** → Tab **Maintenance**
2. **Perform Demo Reset:**
   - Click the "Demo Reset" section
   - Type the word `RESET` in the confirmation dialog
   - The system automatically exports all demo data as a JSON download (backup)
   - All module data is cleared, all users except `admin` are deleted
   - Admin password is reset to `adminpass`, 2FA is disabled
3. **Redirected to the login page** — the yellow banner confirms the reset was completed successfully
4. **Log in with `admin` / `adminpass`** — banner disappears, system is production-ready
5. **Change password immediately:** Settings → Change Password
6. **Set up 2FA:** Settings → Enable 2FA
7. **Create your own users:** Admin Console → Users tab
8. **Create your own content:** all modules are empty and ready to use

### What is preserved after the reset?

| Preserved | Cleared |
|---|---|
| SoA Controls (all 313) | Templates / Policies |
| Dropdown lists | Risks |
| Organisation settings | Assets, BCM, Governance |
| (Admin user) | Suppliers, Legal, Training |
| | GDPR data, Guidance |
| | Audit log, Security Goals |

---

## Restoring Demo Data

If the demo needs to be shown again:

1. **Admin Console → Maintenance → "Import Demo Data"**
2. Select the JSON file downloaded during the demo reset
3. All module data is restored
4. alice and bob are recreated with original passwords and without 2FA
5. The admin account remains unchanged

---

## Further Information

- **Architecture documentation & API reference:** Guidance → Admin-internal
- **Role descriptions:** Guidance → Roles & Responsibilities
- **Project page:** [GitHub – ISMS Builder](https://github.com/claudehecker/isms-builder)
- **License:** GNU Affero General Public License v3.0 (AGPL-3.0)

guidance.json.title: System Manual ISMS Build
guidance.json.content:
# System Manual ISMS Build

## Overview

ISMS Build is a web-based platform for managing Information Security Management System (ISMS) documents. The application supports the full lifecycle of policy documents and integrates GDPR privacy management.

## Core Features

- **Template Management**: Create, edit and version policy documents
- **Lifecycle Management**: draft → review → approved → archived
- **SoA Controls**: Statement of Applicability with 210 controls (ISO27001, BSI, NIS2, EUCS, EUAI)
- **Corporate Structure**: Management of holding company and subsidiaries
- **Risk & Compliance**: Risk register, heatmap, treatment plans, calendar
- **GDPR & Privacy**: RoPA, DPAs, DPIA, data breaches, DSAR, TOMs, DPO (Art. 30/28/35/33/34/15-22/32/37)
- **Reports**: Compliance, framework, gap and audit reports
- **Guidance**: Documentation hub (this section)
- **Calendar**: Aggregated overview of all due actions

## Technology Stack

- **Backend**: Node.js + Express (REST API)
- **Persistence**: JSON files (MVP mode)
- **Auth**: JWT sessions + bcrypt + TOTP 2FA
- **Frontend**: Vanilla JS SPA (Atlassian Dark Theme)

## Sidebar Sections (Order)

| Section | Min Role | Description |
|---------|----------|-------------|
| Dashboard | reader | KPI overview of templates, SoA compliance |
| SoA – Controls | reader | 210 controls, multi-framework, cross-mapping |
| Guidance | reader | Documentation hub, upload, markdown editor |
| Risk & Compliance | editor | Risk register, heatmap, treatment plans |
| GDPR & Privacy | reader | GDPR modules (RoPA, DPA, DPIA, Incidents, DSAR, TOMs, DPO) |
| Admin | admin | RBAC management, users, entities |
| Reports | reader | Compliance, framework, gap, audit reports |
| Calendar | reader | Aggregated calendar of all due dates |
| Settings | reader | User settings, 2FA |

## System Requirements

- Node.js 18+
- Modern browser (Chrome, Firefox, Edge)
- Port 3000 (configurable via .env)

guidance.json.title: Roles & Responsibilities
guidance.json.content:
# Roles & Responsibilities

## Role Model

| Role | Rank | Description | Permissions |
|------|------|-------------|-------------|
| **reader** | 1 | Read access | Read, export |
| **editor** | 2 | Creates and edits | + Create, edit, create RoPA/DSAR |
| **dept_head** | 2 | Department Head | + Department-specific views |
| **contentowner** | 3 | Content Owner | + Approve, manage DPA/DPIA/TOMs/DPO |
| **auditor** | 3 | Audit role | + Create/edit risks and data breaches |
| **admin** | 4 | System Administrator | + Delete, user management, all actions |

## RACI Matrix (incl. GDPR Module)

| Task | Reader | Editor | ContentOwner | Auditor | Admin |
|------|--------|--------|--------------|---------|-------|
| Read RoPA | R | R | R | R | R |
| Create/edit RoPA | — | A | R | R | R |
| Manage DPAs | — | — | A | — | R |
| Manage DPIA | — | — | A | — | R |
| Create data breach | — | — | — | A | R |
| Create/edit DSAR | — | A | R | R | R |
| Manage TOMs | — | — | A | — | R |
| Manage DPO | — | — | A | — | R |
| Create risks | — | — | — | A | R |
| Edit SoA | — | A/C | A | — | R |
| Manage users | — | — | — | — | A |

## Responsibilities in the ISMS Process

1. **ISMS Officer**: ContentOwner role, responsible for policy approval
2. **Data Protection Officer (DPO)**: ContentOwner/Auditor role, maintains the GDPR module
3. **Departments**: Editor role, create department-specific documents and RoPA entries
4. **IT Security**: ContentOwner/Auditor role, maintains SoA, controls, TOMs and risks
5. **Management Board**: Reader role (ContentOwner if required)

templates.json.title: Information Security Policy
templates.json.content: This Information Security Policy defines the framework for protecting Demo Corp information assets. It applies to all employees, contractors and third parties. The policy is aligned with ISO 27001:2022 and reviewed annually.

templates.json.title: Incident Response Procedure
templates.json.content: This procedure defines the process for detecting, classifying, escalating and resolving information security incidents. Severity levels: Critical, High, Medium, Low. Escalation path: Service Desk → CISO → Management.

templates.json.title: Risk Acceptance Policy
templates.json.content: This policy defines the criteria and process for formally accepting residual information security risks. Risk acceptance requires written approval by the risk owner and CISO sign-off for risks rated High or Critical.

templates.json.title: Statement of Applicability (SoA)
templates.json.content: This Statement of Applicability documents all ISO 27001:2022 Annex A controls applicable to Demo Corp, with justification for inclusion or exclusion and implementation status.

vvt.json.title: Customer Management CRM
vvt.json.purpose: Management and maintenance of customer data for contract fulfillment and communication
vvt.json.deletionProcedure: Automatic deletion after expiry of retention period in the CRM system

vvt.json.title: Personnel Administration HR
vvt.json.purpose: Processing of employee data to fulfill employment law obligations and payroll
vvt.json.legalBasisNote: Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR – Legal obligation (§§ 257 HGB, 147 AO)
vvt.json.deletionProcedure: Physical and digital deletion after expiry of statutory retention periods

toms.json.title: Access Control (MFA & RBAC)
toms.json.description: Implementation of multi-factor authentication (MFA) and role-based access control (RBAC) for all systems processing personal data.
toms.json.implementation: MFA via TOTP app for all user accounts. RBAC model with least-privilege principle. Quarterly access reviews.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Evidence: MFA activation rate 100%, RBAC concept v2.1 approved 2024-06-01

toms.json.title: Password Policy (Complexity & Rotation)
toms.json.description: Enforcement of a password policy with minimum length (12 characters), complexity requirements, password history and lockout after failed attempts.
toms.json.implementation: Policy configured in AD/IdP: min. 12 characters, upper/lowercase, digits, special characters. Account lockout after 5 failed attempts (30 min). Password history 12 entries. Password manager recommended.
toms.json.evidenceNote: AD group policy PSO-Standard documented in IT security concept v3

toms.json.title: Privileged Access Management (PAM)
toms.json.description: Management and monitoring of privileged access (admin accounts, service accounts, root access) via a central PAM system.
toms.json.implementation: All admin access via PAM solution (e.g. CyberArk / Vault). Session recording for privileged sessions. Just-in-time provisioning for temporary admin rights. Shared accounts prohibited.
toms.json.evidenceNote: PAM rollout in progress, rollout plan 2025-Q2

toms.json.title: Automatic Screen Lock & Session Timeout
toms.json.description: Automatic locking of workstations after inactivity and automatic expiry of web sessions.
toms.json.implementation: Screen lock after 5 minutes of inactivity (AD group policy). Web applications: session timeout after 30 minutes. JWT token lifetime limited.
toms.json.evidenceNote: AD group policy GPO-Desktop-Security documented

toms.json.title: Visitor Management & Access Control
toms.json.description: Control of physical access to data centres and office areas containing personal data; visitor documentation.
toms.json.implementation: Access control system (smart card) for DC and server rooms. Visitor log (electronic). Escort of visitors by authorised employee. No tailgating possible (airlock).
toms.json.evidenceNote: Access logs retained for 1 year

toms.json.title: Transport Encryption (TLS 1.3)
toms.json.description: All data transmissions over public networks are encrypted with TLS 1.3. Deprecated protocols (TLS 1.0/1.1, SSL) are disabled.
toms.json.implementation: TLS 1.3 configured on all web servers, APIs and email gateways. HSTS enabled. Regular SSL Labs scans (target: A+). STARTTLS enforced for email.
toms.json.evidenceNote: SSL Labs rating: A+ (last checked 2025-01-10)

toms.json.title: Data Encryption at Rest (AES-256)
toms.json.description: Storage of personal data on encrypted storage media and databases (AES-256). Encryption of all mobile devices.
toms.json.implementation: Database level: Transparent Data Encryption (TDE). Storage: BitLocker/FileVault on endpoints. Key management via central KMS. USB storage only permitted if encrypted.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Database servers encrypted. Endpoint rollout approx. 70% complete.

toms.json.title: Email Encryption (S/MIME)
toms.json.description: Encryption of sensitive email communications containing personal data via S/MIME or PGP.
toms.json.implementation: S/MIME certificates issued to all employees with access to sensitive data. Email gateway with TLS enforcement. Policy: no unencrypted transmission of health data or financial information by email.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Pilot project HR department planned 2025-Q3

toms.json.title: Key Management System (KMS)
toms.json.description: Central key management for all cryptographic keys with defined lifecycles, rotation and secure key backup.
toms.json.implementation: Use of a Hardware Security Module (HSM) or KMS service (e.g. AWS KMS / Azure Key Vault). Key rotation: annually or upon compromise. Key backup encrypted at separate location.
toms.json.evidenceNote: KMS concept created, evaluation in progress

toms.json.title: Audit Logging (Access & Actions)
toms.json.description: Logging of all security-relevant events: logins, access to personal data, administrative actions, errors.
toms.json.implementation: Central SIEM system collects logs from all relevant systems. Logs include: timestamp, user, action, affected records, source IP. Log integrity through signing. Retention in accordance with deletion concept.
toms.json.evidenceNote: SIEM system live since 2024-03, log concept documented v1.2
toms.json.retentionRule: Security logs: 1 year (BSI recommendation), access logs: 3 months

toms.json.title: Log Integrity & Tamper Protection
toms.json.description: Protection of log data against unauthorised modification or deletion through technical measures.
toms.json.implementation: Logs are transferred to a separate, read-only SIEM repository. Logs cryptographically signed (hash chaining). Access to raw logs for auditors only. Alert on integrity violation.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Log immutability ensured through SIEM archiving function

toms.json.title: Firewall & Network Segmentation
toms.json.description: Use of firewalls to separate network zones. Systems with personal data in a secured DMZ or isolated network.
toms.json.implementation: Next-generation firewall (NGFW) segments network into zones: internet, DMZ, internal, production, management. Whitelist-based rule sets. Ruleset documented and reviewed semi-annually.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Firewall ruleset last reviewed 2024-10-01, change log available

toms.json.title: VPN for Remote Access
toms.json.description: Encrypted remote access to internal systems exclusively via VPN. Direct internet access to internal servers is blocked.
toms.json.implementation: VPN mandatory for all remote access (home office, business travel). Client certificates + MFA for VPN authentication. Split tunnelling disabled. VPN connection logs retained for 3 months.
toms.json.evidenceNote: VPN usage rate 100% for remote employees, reviewed quarterly

toms.json.title: Intrusion Detection & Prevention (IDS/IPS)
toms.json.description: Automatic detection and defence against attacks and suspicious network activity.
toms.json.implementation: Network IDS/IPS at all network perimeters. Host-based IDS (HIDS) on servers with personal data. Alerting to SIEM. Signatures updated weekly.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Network IDS live, HIDS rollout in progress (60%)

toms.json.title: Web Application Firewall (WAF)
toms.json.description: Protection of web applications against OWASP Top 10 attacks (SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, etc.) via a WAF.
toms.json.implementation: WAF in block mode in front of all public web applications. Ruleset based on OWASP CRS. False-positive rate reviewed monthly. Logs integrated into SIEM.
toms.json.evidenceNote: WAF report Q4/2024: 0 critical vulnerabilities passed through

toms.json.title: Patch Management & Vulnerability Management
toms.json.description: Systematic process for timely identification, assessment and remediation of security vulnerabilities in software and operating systems.
toms.json.implementation: Automated patch deployment for OS and critical applications within 72h after CVSS ≥ 9. Monthly patch cycles for regular patches. Vulnerability scanner weekly. Patch status dashboard for IT management.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Patch compliance rate: >95%, scan report 2025-01

toms.json.title: Penetration Tests & Vulnerability Scans
toms.json.description: Regular external and internal penetration tests and automated vulnerability scans to identify security gaps.
toms.json.implementation: Annual external penetration test by accredited service provider. Semi-annual internal vulnerability scans (Nessus/Qualys). Results transferred to risk register. Retest after remediation.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Last pentest: 2024-09, report available. All critical findings remediated.

toms.json.title: Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL)
toms.json.description: Integration of security and privacy requirements (Privacy by Design, Security by Design) into the software development process.
toms.json.implementation: Mandatory code reviews with security focus. Static code analysis (SAST) in CI/CD pipeline. Dependency scanning for open-source components (SCA). Security stories in every sprint. GDPR checklist for new features.
toms.json.evidenceNote: SAST integrated in CI/CD, SDL training for developers Q1/2025 planned

toms.json.title: Antivirus & Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR)
toms.json.description: Protection of all endpoints against malware, ransomware and other threats via centrally managed AV/EDR solution.
toms.json.implementation: EDR solution (e.g. CrowdStrike / Microsoft Defender for Endpoint) on all endpoints and servers. Real-time protection and behavioural detection. Central management console. Alerts to SIEM. Signatures updated daily.
toms.json.evidenceNote: EDR coverage: 100% of all managed devices

toms.json.title: Backup & Disaster Recovery
toms.json.description: Regular data backup and recovery concept to ensure availability and integrity of personal data.
toms.json.implementation: Daily incremental backups, weekly full backups. Off-site backup in geo-redundant data centre. 3-2-1 backup strategy. Annual DR test with documented RPO/RTO.
toms.json.evidenceNote: DR test 2024-11-15: RPO < 4h, RTO < 8h. Backup logs complete.
toms.json.retentionRule: Backup data: 90 days; backup logs: 1 year

toms.json.title: Backup Restore Tests
toms.json.description: Regular verification of backup recoverability through test restores.
toms.json.implementation: Monthly sample restores from current backups. Quarterly full DR exercise. Results logged and reported to IT management. Backup integrity via automated hash verification.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Restore log 2025-01: all tests successful

toms.json.title: Need-to-Know Principle & Data Access Control
toms.json.description: Access to personal data is granted exclusively to persons who require it for their tasks (purpose limitation, Art. 5(1)(b) GDPR).
toms.json.implementation: Data access concept documented. Department-based access zones. Regular access reviews (quarterly). Role-based permissions in all systems. Approval process for special access.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Data access concept v2.0, last access review 2025-01

toms.json.title: Data Classification
toms.json.description: Classification of all processed data according to protection requirements (public / internal / confidential / strictly confidential) and tailored protective measures.
toms.json.implementation: Data classification policy adopted. Mandatory labelling of documents. Personal data = at least 'confidential'. Special categories (Art. 9 GDPR) = 'strictly confidential'. Training for all employees.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Policy adopted, implementation training Q2/2025 planned

toms.json.title: Incident Response Plan (IRP)
toms.json.description: Documented and tested plan for structured response to data breaches and security incidents (Art. 33/34 GDPR).
toms.json.implementation: IRP document describes roles, escalation paths, 72h reporting obligation, data subject communication. Contact list for supervisory authority (BSI, state data protection authority). Annual tabletop exercise. Supervisory authority notification template in place.
toms.json.evidenceNote: IRP v2.1 approved 2024-11, last tabletop exercise 2024-10

toms.json.title: Business Continuity Plan (BCP)
toms.json.description: Plan for maintaining business operations and availability of personal data during major outages.
toms.json.implementation: BCP documents critical business processes and dependencies. RTO/RPO defined per process. Emergency workstations available. Annual BCP test. Crisis communication plan integrated.
toms.json.evidenceNote: BCP v1.0 created, first test Q2/2025 planned

toms.json.title: Confidentiality Obligation (Employees)
toms.json.description: All employees and external service providers with data access are contractually bound to confidentiality and data protection.
toms.json.implementation: Data protection commitment in employment contract and as separate declaration during onboarding. Externals receive confidentiality agreement (NDA). Annual renewal / acknowledgement of awareness.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Commitment declarations in place for all active employees

toms.json.title: Supplier & Service Provider Management (Third-Party Risk)
toms.json.description: Assessment and management of data protection and security risks from external service providers and suppliers.
toms.json.implementation: Mandatory security questionnaire for all service providers with data access. DPA (Art. 28) before access to personal data. Annual supplier audit or evidence requirement (ISO 27001 certificate). Blacklist for high-risk service providers.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Service provider register maintained, last review 2025-01

toms.json.title: Data Protection Training (Annual)
toms.json.description: Annual mandatory training of all employees on data protection fundamentals (GDPR), data protection-compliant handling and reporting obligations.
toms.json.implementation: E-learning module on internal LMS. Mandatory completion by 31 March each year. Certificate and attendance record stored. New employees: training in onboarding month.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Training plan 2025 created. Start: February 2025.

toms.json.title: IT Security Awareness Training
toms.json.description: Regular security awareness training for all employees on current threats (phishing, social engineering, ransomware).
toms.json.implementation: Annual in-person or e-learning training. Monthly security tips on intranet. Phishing simulations 4× per year. Mandatory for all employees, evidence in HR system.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Participation rate 2024: 98%, report available

toms.json.title: Phishing Simulations
toms.json.description: Simulated phishing attacks to measure and improve the security resilience of the workforce.
toms.json.implementation: 4 simulated phishing campaigns per year (various attack types). Click rate evaluation anonymous/aggregated. Employees who click receive immediate micro-learning. Results reported to management.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Click rate: reduced from 18% (2023) to 6% (2024)
toms.json.retentionRule: Campaign results: 3 years (aggregated, no personal reference)

toms.json.title: Deletion Concept & Deletion Log
toms.json.description: Documented concept for timely and verifiable deletion of personal data after expiry of retention period (Art. 5(1)(e) GDPR).
toms.json.implementation: Deletion concept describes per processing activity: retention period, deletion method, responsible person. Automated deletion processes where possible. Manual deletion review semi-annually. Deletion log archived.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Deletion concept v1.0 created, technical automation implementation in progress

toms.json.title: Data Minimisation & Purpose Limitation
toms.json.description: Ensuring that only the personal data required for the respective purpose is collected and processed (Art. 5(1)(b)(c) GDPR).
toms.json.implementation: Mandatory review: data minimisation as criterion for every new processing activity and feature development. Existing RoPA entries reviewed annually for currency of data fields. Optional fields in forms marked.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Data minimisation check integrated as mandatory item in DPE checklist

toms.json.title: Pseudonymisation & Anonymisation
toms.json.description: Use of pseudonymisation for test data and analytical purposes; anonymisation where personal data is not required for the processing purpose.
toms.json.implementation: Production data is pseudonymised before use in test/development environments. Analytical data anonymised where possible (k-anonymity). Pseudonymisation tool in use. Key stored separately from data set.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Pseudonymisation tool evaluated, rollout for test environments planned Q2/2025

toms.json.title: Storage Media Destruction (DIN 66399)
toms.json.description: Secure and verifiable destruction of storage media (hard drives, USB sticks, paper) pursuant to DIN 66399 before disposal or transfer.
toms.json.implementation: Defective/decommissioned storage media handed over to a certified destruction service provider (security level H-4 for personal data). Destruction certificate issued and retained for 5 years. Paper: shredder level P-4 available.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Destruction certificates 2024 fully archived

toms.json.title: Mobile Device Management (MDM)
toms.json.description: Central management and security of all mobile devices (smartphones, tablets, laptops) with data access.
toms.json.implementation: MDM solution (e.g. Intune / Jamf) for all managed devices. Remote wipe function enabled. Device compliance check before network access (Conditional Access). BYOD policy with container solution for personal devices.
toms.json.evidenceNote: MDM coverage: 100% company devices, BYOD policy v2.0 in force

toms.json.title: Clean Desk & Clean Screen Policy
toms.json.description: Policy for physical security of workstations: no unprotected storage media, locked screens, locked cabinets.
toms.json.implementation: Clean desk policy formally adopted. Regular unannounced audits by IT security. Screen lock automatically after 5 minutes. Printer output to be collected immediately. Confidential documents in lockable cabinets.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Last clean desk audit round: 2024-12, result: 94% compliance

toms.json.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA Process)
toms.json.description: Established process for conducting DPIAs for new processing activities with high risk (Art. 35 GDPR).
toms.json.implementation: DPIA checklist for new projects/features. DPO involvement when risk indicator present. Standardised DPIA template. Decision tree: when is DPIA mandatory (Art. 35(3), supervisory authority blacklists).
toms.json.evidenceNote: DPIA process integrated in project management policy
toms.json.retentionRule: DPIAs: For the duration of processing + 3 years

toms.json.title: Privacy-Friendly Default Settings (Privacy by Default)
toms.json.description: Ensuring that systems and applications use the most privacy-friendly settings by default (Art. 25(2) GDPR).
toms.json.implementation: Privacy by Default checklist for all new systems and features. Opt-in rather than opt-out for optional data processing. Cookies: only technically necessary without consent. No analytics or tracking cookies active by default.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Consent management platform evaluated, implementation Q2/2025

toms.json.title: Secure Disposal of Paper Documents
toms.json.description: Data protection-compliant destruction of paper documents containing personal data.
toms.json.implementation: Shredder security level P-4 (minimum) at all relevant workstations. Privacy container solution for larger volumes with certified destruction. No personal documents in regular waste paper bin.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Privacy containers available, collection by certified service provider

toms.json.title: Server Room & Data Centre Security
toms.json.description: Physical protection of server rooms and data centres against unauthorised access and environmental hazards (fire, water, temperature).
toms.json.implementation: Server rooms with smart card access and PIN. CCTV. Air conditioning with redundancy. Early fire detection system and automatic gas extinguishing system. UPS + emergency generator. Electronic access log.
toms.json.evidenceNote: Last server room security audit: 2024-09, no deficiencies

av.json.title: Microsoft 365 / Azure
av.json.processingScope: Cloud services for email (Exchange Online), documents (SharePoint/OneDrive), video conferencing (Teams) and Azure infrastructure
av.json.notes: Microsoft Data Processing Agreement (DPA) v2024 concluded. Standard contractual clauses pursuant to EU 2021/914.

dsfa.json.title: DPIA: HR Software with Cloud Storage
dsfa.json.processingDescription: Use of a cloud-based HR software (SaaS) for processing employee data including salary data, sick notes and performance appraisals. Data is stored on servers within the EU.
dsfa.json.necessityAssessment: The processing is necessary to fulfill employment law obligations and for efficient HR management. A local alternative would be disproportionately complex and would not increase the level of protection.

risks.description: Unauthorized access by third parties (hacking, data breach at provider)
Data loss due to technical failure

dsfa.json.existingControls: MFA, role-based access control, encrypted transmission (TLS 1.3), daily backups, data processing agreement with provider.
dsfa.json.dpoOpinion: The planned measures are assessed as sufficient. Regular audits at the provider are recommended.
dsfa.json.decisionJustification: The residual risk, taking into account the implemented measures, is to be classified as acceptable. Prior consultation of the supervisory authority is not required.

contracts.json.title: IT Infrastructure Outsourcing – Data Centre Operations
contracts.json.description: Operation and maintenance of on-premise server infrastructure including 24/7 monitoring, SLA Level 2 support and quarterly capacity reviews.

contracts.json.title: Microsoft 365 Enterprise E3 Licensing Agreement
contracts.json.description: Enterprise licence for 350 users covering Exchange Online, Teams, SharePoint and compliance tooling.
contracts.json.notes: Includes Entra ID P1 and Defender for Endpoint.

contracts.json.title: Managed SOC Services – 24/7 Security Monitoring
contracts.json.description: Managed SOC providing 24/7 threat detection, incident triage and monthly reporting including SIEM-as-a-Service.

ndas.json.title: NDA – Penetration Testing Provider 2026
ndas.json.scope: All findings, vulnerabilities and infrastructure information obtained during penetration testing engagements.

ndas.json.title: NDA – ISO 27001 Certification Body
ndas.json.scope: All internal documentation, risk assessments and audit findings shared during Stage 1 and Stage 2 certification audits.

privacy-policies.json.title: Privacy Notice – Website & Online Services
privacy-policies.json.description: Public privacy notice pursuant to Art. 13/14 GDPR for website visitors and users of online services.
privacy-policies.json.content: We process your personal data in accordance with GDPR and BDSG. Controller: Demo GmbH, Musterstraße 1, 40213 Düsseldorf. DPO: dpo@demo.example.

privacy-policies.json.title: Employee Data Protection Policy
privacy-policies.json.description: Internal policy governing processing of employee personal data including HR records, access logs and monitoring.
privacy-policies.json.content: Processing is carried out on the basis of Art. 6(1)(b) and (c) GDPR and § 26 BDSG. Employees have rights of access, rectification and erasure subject to legal retention obligations.
